description: Dialogue for Marcus after being freed from the Crystal Hearts puzzle

nodes.speaker: Marcus
nodes.text: Thank the gods! I thought I would be trapped in that crystal prison forever! The portal... it was a trap. I was so foolish to think I could handle this place alone.

choices.text: Your master sent me to find you.

nodes.speaker: Marcus
nodes.text: Shaken, but unharmed. The crystal preserved me somehow - I could see the world but not move or speak. It was... terrifying. But I'm whole, thanks to you.

nodes.speaker: Marcus
nodes.text: I came seeking rare magical components for my studies. But when I found this chamber, curiosity got the better of me. I touched the central portal and... it consumed me. Trapped me in crystal until someone solved the pillar puzzle.

nodes.speaker: Marcus
nodes.text: Master Aldric? He was worried enough to send someone? I suppose I've been gone far longer than I realized. Time moves strangely in the crystal...

nodes.speaker: Marcus
nodes.text: I owe you my life. I don't know how I can ever repay you. What would you have me do?

choices.text: Come with me. I could use an ally who knows magic.
Return to your master in Dalhurst. He's worried.
Stay here. I don't care what happens to you.

nodes.speaker: Marcus
nodes.text: Travel with you? After what just happened, I... yes. Yes, I think that's exactly what I need. To learn from someone with real experience, not just dusty tomes. I'll follow you wherever you lead.

nodes.speaker: Marcus
nodes.text: I won't let you down. I may not be the strongest fighter, but my knowledge of arcane lore and alchemy could prove useful. Lead on!

nodes.speaker: Marcus
nodes.text: You're right. Master Aldric has taught me so much - I should return and continue my studies properly. Perhaps one day, when I'm stronger, I can repay this debt.

nodes.speaker: Marcus
nodes.text: Thank you again. I'll tell Master Aldric of your heroism. May the Three Gods watch over you!

nodes.speaker: Marcus
nodes.text: What? But... you just freed me! Why would you... No. I see. You're not a hero. You're just someone who stumbled through a puzzle. Fine. I'll find my own way out.

choices.text: Wait... I was testing you. Come with me.

nodes.speaker: Marcus
nodes.text: I'll remember this. We'll meet again, stranger. And next time, the circumstances may not be so... favorable for you.
